steps.title: Identify the defining feature
steps.content: The example illustrates agglomeration through firms that "locate near one another" — establishing that the blank must describe geographic clustering.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the signal
steps.content:
The blank must mean "gather in one place" — firms in the same industry cluster in a region to gain shared benefits. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — recur: happen repeatedly — this describes frequency over time, not spatial clustering in a region. ✗
  • Choice B — dissipate: spread out or scatter — the opposite of clustering; firms that dissipate would move apart and lose the agglomeration benefits. ✗
  • Choice C — concentrate: gather or cluster in one location — directly matches "locate near one another" from the example; firms cluster to share information and spur innovation. ✓
  • Choice D — terminate: end or cease — firms that terminate don't exist to gain economic benefits; this contradicts the passage's premise. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
